commit.message:
feat: add AQ-50 and AQ-10 tests, fix ASRS 18-item integration

- Add AQ-50 (Baron-Cohen et al., 2001): 50 items, binary scoring, 5 subscales (SOC/ATT/DET/COM/IMG), cutoff ≥32
- Add AQ-10 (Allison et al., 2012): 10 items, binary scoring, cutoff ≥6
- Create AQResults.jsx and AQ10Results.jsx components with subscale charts and item breakdowns
- Fix ASRS_SUBSCALES import in App.jsx (was missing, caused runtime reference error)
- Fix PatientProfile maxScores: asrs 24→18, add aq:50, aq10:10
- Add aq/aq10 to TEST_META, getTestStatus, and history color coding
- Fix single-quote escape in aq.js Czech question

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2: Fix scraper extracting platform-level links instead of individual job URLs

Problem: v1's generic extractor grabbed navigation links ("产品与技术"),
category labels ("技术运营类"), and JD text fragments as job entries.
ByteDance returned 0 real jobs, Tencent had no URLs, MiniMax had 30+
duplicate fragments.

Fix:
- Add platform-specific extractors for Feishu, ByteDance, Tencent
- URL pattern filtering: only keep /position/{ID}, /job_detail/{ID} etc
- Click-through for SPA cards that use click handlers instead of <a> tags
- Auto-pagination (default 3 pages)
- Proper deduplication by URL or title
- Structured field extraction (title, location, department, jobType)

New difficulty section added to README (Difficulty #8).

commit.message:
fix: strip non-existent columns from jobsFields to match production DB

The supabase_schema.sql is a reference file that doesn't match production.
Columns like contract_amount, labor_cost, material_cost, billing_type,
permit/inspection/warranty fields were never deployed. Stripped jobsFields
to only include columns verified via schema_fixes.sql and app usage:
job_id, job_title, status, business_unit, job_address, start_date, end_date,
assigned_team, allotted_time_hours, time_tracked, utility_incentive,
discount, expense_amount, profit_margin, invoice_status, recurrence,
details, notes, gps_location, plus virtual customer_name.

commit.message:
feat: deduplicate NVD CVE lookups for severity enrichment (#6)

* feat: deduplicate CVE lookups in NVD severity enrichment

Many deb/distro packages share the same CVE (e.g., libssl3, openssl,
libssl-dev all reference CVE-2024-XXXX). Previously, enrich_vulnerabilities()
made one NVD API call per vulnerability, causing redundant requests and
hitting the 5 req/30s rate limit unnecessarily.

Now: extract unique CVE IDs before fetching, fetch each once, and apply
the CVSS data to all vulns sharing that CVE. Also adds proper batch
pacing (5 concurrent per batch without API key, 40 with key) with
rate-limit pauses between batches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove unused import and variable to fix ruff lint errors

Remove unused AsyncMock import (F401) and unused original_get_cve
variable (F841) in tests/test_databases.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use HTTPS for Splunk HEC endpoint in lab Alloy config

Splunk's official Docker image enforces HTTPS on port 8088 even in lab
mode — HTTP connections are silently reset at the TCP layer. Switch the
otelcol.exporter.splunkhec endpoint from http:// to https:// and rely
on insecure_skip_verify=true (already set) to accept the self-signed cert.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: revert to V2 ABIs matching deployed contracts

The deployed Quinty and Quest contracts on Base Sepolia are V2, but the
frontend was upgraded to V3 ABIs (with prizes[], token, pull withdrawals)
without redeploying the contracts. This caused all createBounty and
createQuest transactions to revert on-chain due to function selector
mismatch.

- Restore V2 ABI files (5-param createBounty, 6-param createQuest)
- Fix all contract call sites to use V2 signatures
- Fix getBounty/getQuest destructuring (no token field in V2)
- Add socialHandle param to submitToBounty/submitEntry (V2 requirement)
- Replace selectWinners with selectWinner (V2 single winner)
- Remove ERC-20 approval flows (V2 is ETH-only)
- Disable withdrawal hook (V2 uses push payments, not pull)

commit.message:
fix(play): correct diversity-queue exclude logic and O(n) claim lookup

The exclude guard in buildDiverseQueue used `idx > 0` which silently
skipped exclusion when the last-played category landed at shuffled
index 0 — causing same-category starts ~50% of the time with 2
categories. Fixed to `idx >= 0`.

Replaced O(n) segments.find() per verdict in results phase with a
pre-built Map<string, ParsedClaim> for O(1) claim lookups.

commit.message:
fix: allow github app reinstall on different organization (#3832)

* fix: allow github app reinstall on different organization

Previously, after uninstalling a GitHub integration from our DB,
the install endpoint would discover the old installation via GitHub
API and automatically re-create the DB record, preventing users from
reaching GitHub's install page to choose a new organization.

Now tryLinkFromGitHubApi only links users to installations already
tracked in our DB, letting uninstalled users proceed to GitHub's
organization selection page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: complete github app reinstall flow with api uninstall and auto-linking

- Delete installation from GitHub API on uninstall so reinstall triggers fresh callback
- Restore auto-create logic in tryLinkFromGitHubApi for installations missing from DB
- Pass knownGithubUserId to linkVm0User for User-type installations
- Add TUNNEL_HOSTNAME to turbo.json env passthrough

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent non-admin users from deleting github app installation

When adminGithubUserId is null (e.g. org install where admin hasn't
been set), the comparison `null !== null` evaluates to false, letting
any linked user pass the admin check. Now require adminGithubUserId
to be non-null in DELETE, PATCH, and GET isAdmin checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: don't redirect when user link creation fails in install endpoint

When linkVm0User returns null (user has no GitHub OAuth connector),
don't short-circuit to the settings page. Instead fall through to the
GitHub redirect so the user can go through the proper install flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings

- replace non-null assertion with explicit null check in tryLinkFromGitHubApi
- change listAppInstallations error log from error to warn (best-effort detection)
- add tests for admin null-check security fix (DELETE and PATCH with null adminGithubUserId)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix follow mode bypass: handle follow before sweep, remove duplicate

commit.message:
fix: address code review findings — security and correctness bugs

- Slack approval callbacks (robodev_approve_* / robodev_reject_*) are
  now acknowledged and logged only; they are no longer forwarded to
  ProcessTicket, which was creating spurious TaskRuns with action IDs
  as ticket IDs.

- GitHub polling backend filters out pull requests returned by the
  /issues endpoint via the pull_request field on ghIssue.

- GitHub webhook now enforces the trigger-label contract: a new
  trigger_labels option on webhook.github mirrors the polling backend's
  label-gating so arbitrary newly-opened issues cannot trigger execution.

- webhookAdapter.HandleWebhookEvent returns the first ProcessTicket
  error rather than always nil, so senders receive a non-2xx status and
  will retry failed deliveries.

- Code review gate is now an actual gate: when ReviewDiff returns
  Passed=false the TaskRun transitions to Failed and the ticket is
  marked failed instead of silently succeeding.

- IntelligentSelector.SelectEngines guards against a nil fallback
  (cold-start panic path) by returning the available engine list in order.

- Routing only advertises engines whose configuration is non-nil;
  previously all keys in the map were added regardless of value.

- Correct wrong config key names in README and docs:
  max_cost_per_task_usd → max_cost_per_job,
  max_duration_minutes → max_job_duration_minutes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
